pi_items.source: Task specification and setup scripts define this filename as the output target
Task specification and setup scripts define this filename as the PNG export target
Task description explicitly requires 3 pages: System Context (Page 1), Container (Page 2), Component (Page 3)

Verified against official Mastodon documentation (docs.joinmastodon.org) and technical architecture sources. Keywords accurately represent Mastodon's system context: Mastodon is the platform, users interact with it, it federates via ActivityPub protocol (fediverse), sends email notifications, and stores media in object storage

Verified against official Mastodon documentation and production deployment patterns. Web=Ruby on Rails application, streaming=Node.js WebSocket API, sidekiq=background job processor, postgres=primary database, redis=cache and job queue backend, elastic=Elasticsearch (optional full-text search), nginx=reverse proxy/load balancer used in production deployments

Verified against Mastodon's Rails application architecture. These represent core functional components: Authentication (Devise/OAuth2), REST API, ActivityPub protocol handler for federation, media file handling, timeline aggregation (home/local/federated), notification service, account/user management, and status/post management

privileged_info_summary: The specification describes the actual technical architecture of the real Mastodon social network platform, not a synthetic system. Mastodon uses a Ruby on Rails backend for the web application, Node.js for streaming API (WebSocket), PostgreSQL for the primary database, Redis for caching and as the backend for Sidekiq background job processing, Elasticsearch (optional) for full-text search, and Nginx as a reverse proxy in production. The system implements ActivityPub protocol for federation with other Fediverse instances, supports email notifications, and stores user media in object storage. Key functional components in the Rails application include: Authentication (Devise/OAuth2), REST API controllers, ActivityPub protocol handling, media attachment handler, timeline aggregator for building home/local/federated feeds, notification service for mentions and follows, account manager for user profiles, and status manager for post creation/deletion.
